title: The Present Perfect Tense
description: Plain-English lesson on the pretérito perfecto: haber + past participle, regular and irregular participles, with formation drills.

lessons.summary: The present perfect (pretérito perfecto) is a compound tense — two words: the present tense of haber + the past participle. Use it for the recent past and for past events that still matter now ('¿Has comido?' / '¿Has estado en España?'). Haber here is an auxiliary meaning 'to have'; it is never used like tener — you can't say 'yo he un libro' (that's 'tengo un libro'). Forms of haber: he, has, ha, hemos, habéis, han. Regular participles: -ar → -ado, -er/-ir → -ido.

examples.spanish: He vivido acá por muchos años.
examples.english: I have lived here for many years.
Have you eaten? — Yes, I have.

examples.note: perfect (relevance now) vs preterite '¿Viste a Tom?'

items.prompt: Yo ___ vivido acá por muchos años. (haber)
items.promptEnglish: I have lived here for many years.
items.explanation: Present perfect = present of haber + participle. For 'yo', haber is 'he'.

items.promptEnglish: Have you seen my red book?
He has done everything for you.

items.explanation: -er verbs form the participle with -ido: comer → comido.
-ar verbs form the participle with -ado: hablar → hablado.

items.prompt: Yo no ___ el vaso. (romper — 'I have not broken')
items.promptEnglish: I have not broken the glass.
items.explanation: he (haber, yo) + roto (irregular participle of romper).
han (haber, ustedes) + dicho (irregular participle of decir).

items.prompt: Ellas todavía no ___ la carta. (escribir — 'have written')
items.promptEnglish: They still haven't written the letter.
items.explanation: han (haber, ellas) + escrito (irregular participle of escribir).

items.prompt: Which sentence is in the present perfect?
items.explanation: The present perfect uses haber + participle (has visto). '¿Viste?' is the preterite (simple past).

items.prompt: Escribe una frase en pretérito perfecto sobre algo que has hecho hoy.
items.promptEnglish: Write a present-perfect sentence about something you've done today.
items.guidance: Must be grammatical Spanish using the present perfect: present of haber (he/has/ha/hemos/han) + a correct past participle, e.g. 'Hoy he estudiado español.' Mark incorrect if it uses the preterite (e.g. 'estudié') or an incorrect participle.
items.explanation: Present perfect = present of haber + past participle, for the recent past.
